_comment: Break pricing and odds reference for 2025-26 Topps NBA Hoops Basketball. Checklist not yet available.

breakPricingGuidance._comment: Break pricing guidance. All prices in USD. Based on single-case economics.
breakPricingGuidance.caseNote: 8-box hobby jumbo case (Hobby Jumbo configuration). 16 autos/case avg. Budget-friendly product. Released May 14, 2026. Good entry-level break product with strong collector base.

pytPricing._comment: PYT = Pick Your Team. 30 spots (one per NBA team). Revenue target: $3,795. Avg spot: $126.

randomBreak._comment: Random break — equal spots. 30 spots at $125/spot = $3,750.

breakPricingGuidance.scalingNote: For multi-case breaks, multiply all revenue targets by number of cases. Margin logic stays the same.

evFramework._comment: How to price individual spots against card comps (EV analysis).
evFramework.step1: Pull 30-day sold comps on eBay for each team's key cards (top parallel, RC auto if applicable).
evFramework.step2: Sum the EV across all cards a buyer would realistically pull from their team allocation.
evFramework.step3: Set spot price = EV × (1 - breaker_margin). A fair spot is one where buyer EV ≥ spot price at breakeven, breaker earns margin.
evFramework.step4: Premium teams (Dallas w/ Flagg, SA w/ Wemby) can be priced at 2-4× average because buyers will pay a scarcity premium for the highest-demand cards.
evFramework.step5: Validate: sum of all spot prices must ≥ revenue target. If not, raise lowest-demand spots.
